# @jsondb-cloud/client — Full API Reference

> Official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for jsondb.cloud — a hosted JSON document database.

## Install

```bash
npm install @jsondb-cloud/client
```

## Exports

```typescript
// Classes
import { JsonDB, Collection, QueryBuilder, ChangeStream } from "@jsondb-cloud/client";

// Error classes
import {
  JsonDBError, NotFoundError, ConflictError, ValidationError,
  UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError, QuotaExceededError,
  RateLimitError, DocumentTooLargeError, ServerError,
} from "@jsondb-cloud/client";

// Types
import type {
  Document, DocumentMeta, ListResponse, ListOptions, FilterOperators, FilterValue,
  BulkOperation, BulkResult, JsonDBConfig, StreamOptions, StreamEventType,
  StreamEventData, VersionEntry, VersionDiff, Webhook, WebhookCreateOptions,
  WebhookUpdateOptions, WebhookDelivery, WebhookWithDeliveries,
  ImportOptions, ImportResult, ExportOptions,
} from "@jsondb-cloud/client";
```

---

## JsonDB Client

### Constructor

```typescript
const db = new JsonDB(config: JsonDBConfig);
```

#### JsonDBConfig

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `apiKey` | `string` | — | **Required.** API key (`jdb_sk_live_...` or `jdb_sk_test_...`) |
| `project` | `string` | `"v1"` | Project identifier |
| `baseUrl` | `string` | `"https://api.jsondb.cloud"` | API base URL |
| `timeout` | `number` | `30000` | Request timeout in milliseconds |
| `retry.enabled` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable automatic retry with exponential backoff |
| `retry.maxRetries` | `number` | `3` | Maximum retry attempts |
| `retry.baseDelay` | `number` | `1000` | Base delay in ms (doubles each retry) |
| `retry.maxDelay` | `number` | `10000` | Maximum delay cap in ms |
| `headers` | `Record<string, string>` | `{}` | Extra headers for every request |
| `fetch` | `typeof fetch` | `globalThis.fetch` | Custom fetch implementation |

### db.collection(name)

```typescript
const users = db.collection<{ name: string; email: string }>("users");
```

Returns a typed `Collection<T>` instance. The generic parameter `T` defines the shape of your documents (excluding metadata fields).

### db.listCollections()

```typescript
const collections: string[] = await db.listCollections();
// ["users", "posts", "comments"]
```

---

## Collection

All methods below are on `Collection<T>`.

### Document Type

Every stored document has these metadata fields added automatically:

```typescript
interface DocumentMeta {
  _id: string;        // Unique document ID
  $createdAt: string; // ISO 8601 timestamp
  $updatedAt: string; // ISO 8601 timestamp
  $version: number;   // Version number
}

type Document<T> = T & DocumentMeta;
```

---

### CRUD Operations

#### create(doc, options?)

Create a new document. Returns the created document with metadata.

```typescript
// Auto-generated ID
const alice = await users.create({ name: "Alice", email: "alice@example.com" });
// alice._id = "auto-generated-id"

// Explicit ID
const bob = await users.create({ name: "Bob", email: "bob@example.com" }, { id: "bob-1" });
// bob._id = "bob-1"
```

**Parameters:**
- `doc: T` — The document body
- `options?: { id?: string }` — Optional explicit document ID

**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`

#### get(id)

Get a single document by ID.

```typescript
const user = await users.get("alice-1");
// { _id: "alice-1", name: "Alice", email: "...", $createdAt: "...", $updatedAt: "...", $version: 1 }
```

**Parameters:** `id: string`
**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`
**Throws:** `NotFoundError` if document doesn't exist

#### list(options?)

List documents with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination.

```typescript
const result = await users.list({
  filter: { role: "admin" },
  sort: "-$createdAt",
  limit: 10,
  offset: 0,
  select: ["name", "email"],
});
// result.data = [{ _id, name, email, ... }, ...]
// result.meta = { total: 42, limit: 10, offset: 0, hasMore: true }
```

**Parameters:** `ListOptions<T>`

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `filter` | `Record<string, FilterValue>` | Field filters (see Filtering below) |
| `sort` | `string` | Sort field. Prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. `"-$createdAt"`) |
| `limit` | `number` | Max documents to return |
| `offset` | `number` | Skip this many documents |
| `select` | `string[]` | Only return these fields |

**Returns:** `Promise<ListResponse<T>>`

```typescript
interface ListResponse<T> {
  data: Document<T>[];
  meta: { total: number; limit: number; offset: number; hasMore: boolean };
}
```

#### Filtering

Filters support direct equality or operator objects:

```typescript
// Equality
{ filter: { status: "active" } }

// Operators
{ filter: { age: { $gt: 18, $lt: 65 } } }
```

**Available operators:**

| Operator | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `$eq` | Equal | `{ status: { $eq: "active" } }` |
| `$neq` | Not equal | `{ status: { $neq: "deleted" } }` |
| `$gt` | Greater than | `{ age: { $gt: 18 } }` |
| `$gte` | Greater than or equal | `{ age: { $gte: 18 } }` |
| `$lt` | Less than | `{ age: { $lt: 65 } }` |
| `$lte` | Less than or equal | `{ age: { $lte: 65 } }` |
| `$contains` | String contains | `{ name: { $contains: "ali" } }` |
| `$in` | Value in array | `{ role: { $in: ["admin", "mod"] } }` |
| `$exists` | Field exists | `{ avatar: { $exists: true } }` |

#### count(filter?)

Count documents matching an optional filter.

```typescript
const total = await users.count();
const admins = await users.count({ role: "admin" });
```

**Parameters:** `filter?: ListOptions<T>["filter"]`
**Returns:** `Promise<number>`

#### update(id, doc)

Replace a document entirely (PUT semantics).

```typescript
await users.update("alice-1", { name: "Alice Smith", email: "alice@newdomain.com" });
```

**Parameters:** `id: string`, `doc: T`
**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`

#### patch(id, partial)

Partial update using merge-patch (only provided fields are changed).

```typescript
await users.patch("alice-1", { email: "alice@newdomain.com" });
// Only email is updated; name and other fields preserved
```

**Parameters:** `id: string`, `partial: Partial<T>`
**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`
**Content-Type:** `application/merge-patch+json`

#### jsonPatch(id, operations)

Apply JSON Patch operations per RFC 6902.

```typescript
await users.jsonPatch("alice-1", [
  { op: "replace", path: "/name", value: "Alice Smith" },
  { op: "add", path: "/verified", value: true },
  { op: "remove", path: "/tempField" },
]);
```

**Parameters:**
- `id: string`
- `operations: { op: string; path: string; value?: unknown; from?: string }[]`

**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`
**Content-Type:** `application/json-patch+json`

#### delete(id)

Delete a document.

```typescript
await users.delete("alice-1");
```

**Parameters:** `id: string`
**Returns:** `Promise<void>`

---

### Query Builder

Fluent interface for building queries. Start with `where()` on a collection.

```typescript
const result = await users
  .where("age", ">=", 18)
  .where("status", "==", "active")
  .orderBy("-name")
  .limit(10)
  .offset(5)
  .select("name", "age")
  .exec();
```

**Operators:** `==`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`, `contains`, `in`

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `where(field, op, value)` | Add a filter condition (chainable) |
| `orderBy(field)` | Sort results. Prefix `-` for descending |
| `limit(n)` | Limit result count |
| `offset(n)` | Skip results |
| `select(...fields)` | Pick specific fields |
| `exec()` | Returns `Promise<ListResponse<T>>` |

---

### Bulk Operations

#### bulk(operations)

Mixed bulk operations in a single request.

```typescript
const result = await users.bulk([
  { method: "POST", body: { name: "Alice" } },
  { method: "PUT", id: "bob-1", body: { name: "Bob Updated" } },
  { method: "PATCH", id: "charlie-1", body: { verified: true } },
  { method: "DELETE", id: "old-user-1" },
]);
// result.summary = { total: 4, succeeded: 4, failed: 0 }
```

**Parameters:** `BulkOperation[]`

```typescript
interface BulkOperation {
  method: "POST" | "PUT" | "PATCH" | "DELETE";
  id?: string;
  body?: Record<string, unknown>;
}
```

**Returns:** `Promise<BulkResult>`

```typescript
interface BulkResult {
  results: { status: number; _id: string; ok: boolean; error?: string }[];
  summary: { total: number; succeeded: number; failed: number };
}
```

#### bulkCreate(docs)

Shorthand for bulk-creating multiple documents.

```typescript
const result = await users.bulkCreate([
  { name: "Alice" },
  { name: "Bob" },
  { name: "Charlie" },
]);
```

**Parameters:** `docs: T[]`
**Returns:** `Promise<BulkResult>`

---

### Schema Validation

#### setSchema(schema)

Set a JSON Schema for this collection. All future writes are validated against it.

```typescript
await users.setSchema({
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string", minLength: 1 },
    email: { type: "string", format: "email" },
    age: { type: "integer", minimum: 0 },
  },
  required: ["name", "email"],
});
```

**Parameters:** `schema: Record<string, unknown>` (JSON Schema object)
**Returns:** `Promise<void>`

#### getSchema()

Get the current schema for this collection.

```typescript
const schema = await users.getSchema();
// null if no schema is set
```

**Returns:** `Promise<Record<string, unknown> | null>`

#### removeSchema()

Remove the schema from this collection.

```typescript
await users.removeSchema();
```

**Returns:** `Promise<void>`

#### validate(doc)

Validate a document against the collection schema without storing it.

```typescript
const result = await users.validate({ name: "", email: "not-an-email" });
// { valid: false, errors: [{ path: "/name", message: "must NOT have fewer than 1 characters", keyword: "minLength" }] }
```

**Parameters:** `doc: T`
**Returns:** `Promise<{ valid: boolean; errors: { path: string; message: string; keyword: string }[] }>`

---

### Version History

#### listVersions(id)

List all versions of a document.

```typescript
const { versions } = await users.listVersions("alice-1");
// [{ version: 1, action: "create", timestamp: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", size: 128 }, ...]
```

**Returns:** `Promise<{ versions: VersionEntry[] }>`

```typescript
interface VersionEntry {
  version: number;
  action: string;
  timestamp: string;
  size?: number;
}
```

#### getVersion(id, version)

Get a document at a specific version.

```typescript
const oldDoc = await users.getVersion("alice-1", 1);
```

**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`

#### restoreVersion(id, version)

Restore a document to a specific version. Creates a new version with the old content.

```typescript
const restored = await users.restoreVersion("alice-1", 1);
```

**Returns:** `Promise<Document<T>>`

#### diffVersions(id, from, to)

Diff two versions of a document.

```typescript
const diff = await users.diffVersions("alice-1", 1, 3);
// { added: { verified: true }, removed: {}, changed: { name: { from: "Alice", to: "Alice Smith" } } }
```

**Returns:** `Promise<VersionDiff>`

```typescript
interface VersionDiff {
  added: Record<string, unknown>;
  removed: Record<string, unknown>;
  changed: Record<string, { from: unknown; to: unknown }>;
}
```

---

### Webhooks

#### createWebhook(options)

Register a webhook on this collection.

```typescript
const webhook = await users.createWebhook({
  url: "https://example.com/webhook",
  events: ["document.created", "document.updated"],
  description: "Sync new users",
  secret: "whsec_xxxx", // Optional HMAC signing secret
});
```

**Parameters:** `WebhookCreateOptions`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | Endpoint URL |
| `events` | `("document.created" \| "document.updated" \| "document.deleted")[]` | Yes | Events to listen for |
| `description` | `string` | No | Human-readable description |
| `secret` | `string` | No | HMAC signing secret |

**Returns:** `Promise<Webhook>`

#### listWebhooks()

```typescript
const { data } = await users.listWebhooks();
```

**Returns:** `Promise<{ data: Webhook[] }>`

#### getWebhook(webhookId)

Get webhook details including recent deliveries.

```typescript
const webhook = await users.getWebhook("wh_xxxx");
// webhook.recentDeliveries = [{ _id, webhookId, timestamp, statusCode, responseTime }, ...]
```

**Returns:** `Promise<WebhookWithDeliveries>`

#### updateWebhook(webhookId, options)

```typescript
await users.updateWebhook("wh_xxxx", {
  url: "https://new-endpoint.com/hook",
  status: "disabled",
});
```

**Parameters:** `WebhookUpdateOptions` — all fields optional: `url`, `events`, `description`, `status`
**Returns:** `Promise<Webhook>`

#### deleteWebhook(webhookId)

```typescript
await users.deleteWebhook("wh_xxxx");
```

**Returns:** `Promise<void>`

#### testWebhook(webhookId)

Send a test event to a webhook.

```typescript
const delivery = await users.testWebhook("wh_xxxx");
// { _id: "del_xxxx", webhookId: "wh_xxxx", statusCode: 200, responseTime: 150, timestamp: "..." }
```

**Returns:** `Promise<WebhookDelivery>`

---

### Import / Export

#### importDocuments(documents, options?)

Import documents into this collection.

```typescript
const result = await users.importDocuments(
  [{ name: "Alice" }, { name: "Bob" }],
  { onConflict: "skip" },
);
```

**Parameters:**
- `documents: Record<string, unknown>[]`
- `options?: ImportOptions`

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `onConflict` | `"fail" \| "skip" \| "overwrite"` | Behavior on ID conflict |
| `idField` | `string` | Use this field as the document ID |

**Returns:** `Promise<ImportResult>`

```typescript
interface ImportResult {
  results: { status: number; document?: Record<string, unknown>; error?: string }[];
}
```

#### exportDocuments(options?)

Export all documents from this collection.

```typescript
const docs = await users.exportDocuments();
const admins = await users.exportDocuments({ filter: { role: "admin" } });
```

**Parameters:** `options?: ExportOptions` — optional `filter` object
**Returns:** `Promise<Record<string, unknown>[]>`

---

### Real-time Streaming (SSE)

Subscribe to real-time changes via Server-Sent Events.

```typescript
const stream = users.stream({ events: ["created", "updated"] });

stream.on("created", (doc, raw) => {
  console.log("New user:", doc.name);
});

stream.on("updated", (doc, raw) => {
  console.log("Updated:", doc._id);
});

stream.on("deleted", (id, raw) => {
  console.log("Deleted:", id);
});

stream.on("change", (data, raw) => {
  console.log("Any change:", data.event);
});

stream.on("error", (err) => console.error(err));
stream.on("open", () => console.log("Connected"));

// Stop listening
stream.close();
```

**Parameters:** `StreamOptions`

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `events` | `("created" \| "updated" \| "deleted")[]` | Filter which events to receive |
| `filter` | `Record<string, string \| number \| boolean>` | Filter by document fields |

**Events:**

| Event | Handler Signature | Description |
|-------|-------------------|-------------|
| `created` | `(doc: T & { _id: string }, raw: StreamEventData) => void` | Document created |
| `updated` | `(doc: T & { _id: string }, raw: StreamEventData) => void` | Document updated |
| `deleted` | `(id: string, raw: StreamEventData) => void` | Document deleted |
| `change` | `(data: StreamEventData, raw: StreamEventData) => void` | Any change |
| `error` | `(error: unknown) => void` | Connection error |
| `open` | `() => void` | Connection opened |

The stream automatically reconnects with exponential backoff (1s to 30s) and resumes from the last event ID.

---

## Error Handling

All API errors extend `JsonDBError`:

```typescript
class JsonDBError extends Error {
  code: string;                      // Machine-readable error code
  status: number;                    // HTTP status code
  details: Record<string, unknown>;  // Additional error context
}
```

### Error Classes

| Class | Status | Code | Extra Fields |
|-------|--------|------|-------------|
| `NotFoundError` | 404 | `DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND` | `documentId?: string` |
| `ValidationError` | 400 | `VALIDATION_FAILED` | `errors: { path, message, keyword }[]` |
| `ConflictError` | 409 | `CONFLICT` | — |
| `UnauthorizedError` | 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | — |
| `ForbiddenError` | 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | — |
| `RateLimitError` | 429 | `RATE_LIMITED` | — |
| `QuotaExceededError` | 429 | `QUOTA_EXCEEDED` | — |
| `DocumentTooLargeError` | 413 | `DOCUMENT_TOO_LARGE` | — |
| `ServerError` | 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | — |

### Example

```typescript
import { JsonDB, NotFoundError, ValidationError, RateLimitError } from "@jsondb-cloud/client";

try {
  await users.create({ name: "" });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ValidationError) {
    console.log("Validation errors:", err.errors);
    // [{ path: "/name", message: "must NOT have fewer than 1 characters", keyword: "minLength" }]
  } else if (err instanceof NotFoundError) {
    console.log("Not found:", err.documentId);
  } else if (err instanceof RateLimitError) {
    console.log("Rate limited, retry later");
  }
}
```

---

## Retry Behavior

The SDK automatically retries on:
- HTTP 429 (rate limit / quota)
- HTTP 5xx (server errors)
- Network errors

Retry uses exponential backoff: `baseDelay * 2^attempt`, capped at `maxDelay`.

Disable retries:
```typescript
const db = new JsonDB({ apiKey: "...", retry: { enabled: false } });
```

---

## TypeScript Generics

Collections are strongly typed via generics:

```typescript
interface User {
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
}

const users = db.collection<User>("users");

// All methods are typed:
const alice = await users.create({ name: "Alice", email: "a@b.com", age: 30 }); // OK
await users.create({ invalid: true }); // Type error

// Returned documents include metadata:
alice._id;        // string
alice.$createdAt; // string
alice.$version;   // number
alice.name;       // string
```

## Links

- Docs: https://jsondb.cloud/docs/sdks/node
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jsondb-cloud/client
- GitHub: https://github.com/JsonDBCloud/node

## Related Packages

- `@jsondb-cloud/mcp` — MCP server for AI agents — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jsondb-cloud/mcp
- `@jsondb-cloud/cli` — CLI tool — https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jsondb-cloud/cli
- `jsondb-cloud` (PyPI) — Python SDK — https://pypi.org/project/jsondb-cloud/
